Poor quality due to recording being made using whatsapp voice-note function. 
Bird responding to whistled impression of call, later flew in to investigate and observed using flashlight.

At least two Philippine Eagle-owls calling each other. One owl clearly seen and photographed in flashlight. Observed owl flew in and sat on a sidebranch of a Benguet pine tree, c. 4 m. above the ground. Restless (perhaps also because of observers) but remained on the branch for at least 2 minutes (then we left the owl alone). 
Recording: iphone 11 recording with built-in microphone and Rode Rec app. File shortened and amplified using Audacity.

Morning walk through the village. A collared kingfisher is usually hanging out around a large pond with plenty of fish. Calling sitting on a power line. Cear morning with no wind and 25C. Noise reduction in Audacity 12dB and highpass filter 6dB/650Hz.

We have a rather large colony close to our place where a gravel/sand bank extends from the road to the rice field. Around 50 birds in total probably. They are very active and chatty when sitting on telephone lines or feeding over the fields.

During a morning walk around the area. Recorded at the edge of a small village by some rice fields, close to the South china sea. A calm morning with clear skies and nice 20C temp. 

Uodated: Originally a mystery bird: Sounds like a Locustella of some kind singing at 4.5 secs and 27 sec.

Early morning in a small village beween rice fields and the ocean. 8dB Noise reduction and 650kHz highpassfilter.
Three high pitched calls, each with three syllables "ti-ti-tii".

During a morning walk around the area. Recorded in a small village with scattered small kubos, close to the South china sea. A calm morning with clear skies and nice 20C temp. Bird, probably a Zosterops of some sort,  was singing from a dense tree canopy in a garden. Lowland white-eye (Zosterops meyeni) is abundant in the area but I don't think I ever heard them singing.

Edit: confirmed Lowland.
